meta.title: Server vs Client Components
meta.excerpt: Understanding the differences between server and client components in Next.js and when to use each.

seo.metaTitle: Server vs Client Components | App Reference
seo.metaDescription: Learn when to use server vs client components in Next.js applications.

blocks.content: Next.js defaults to server components for performance, with opt-in client components for interactivity.

blocks.title: Use Client Directive
blocks.content: Mark a component with 'use client' at the top of the file to opt into client-side rendering and enable interactivity.
Choose server components by default for better performance. Use client components only when you need interactivity or browser APIs.

features.title: Use Server For
features.description: Data fetching, API calls, database access, large dependencies

features.title: Use Client For
features.description: Event handlers, useState, useEffect, browser APIs

features.title: Hybrid Approach
features.description: Server component wrapping client components for data

features.title: Performance
features.description: Server components reduce JavaScript bundle size

blocks.title: Decision Matrix
Default to Server
blocks.content: Always start with server components and only add 'use client' when you encounter an error or need interactivity.
Compose server and client components effectively to maximize performance while maintaining interactivity.

blocks.title: Server Wrapper Pattern
blocks.content: Server component fetches data and passes to client component as props

blocks.title: Children Composition
blocks.content: Pass server components as children to client components

blocks.title: Shared Components
blocks.content: Pure presentational components can be used in both contexts

blocks.title: Server Wrapper Pattern Example
Best Practice
blocks.content: Keep client components small and focused. Let server components handle data fetching and pass serializable props to client components.
